title: Fix publication final_sha to advertise close-record commit
goal: Eliminate the evidentiary gap in PUB-01's publication seam: final_sha must point at the post-publication integration HEAD that includes close records and reports, not the pre-publication implementation merge commit.
acceptance: Handoff, final-report, and implementation-summary final_sha all point at the integration branch commit that includes closed slice JSON, final report artifacts, and implementation changes together.

The failed run kd-20260706-215228-0f3bba96 is covered as a regression shape: final_sha 9a0eb84594c7c26edbcd648c8f807c249bf4ce08 was unacceptable because the integration branch tip was 1d3f90c544a783e627faa83b25efce7333f967dc and only the tip contained PUB-01A closed metadata plus run reports.

A regression test asserts final_sha equals git rev-parse INTEGRATION_BRANCH and verifies the commit at that SHA contains status=closed with closed_by_run matching the run ID plus the run's committed final-report and implementation-summary files under .workflow/reports/.
Branch-level handoff commands remain valid: the integration branch tip still carries implementation + close records + reports together.
Existing PUB-01 handoff, incident, resume, and close-metadata tests remain green under the full verification profile.
must_ask_if: The implementation would change the handoff command shape or require operator replan/approval for normal successful publication.
The implementation would add new run or slice terminal statuses.
The implementation would move final_sha to a second commit or require post-publication rebase instead of fixing the ordering in one commit.
The implementation cannot satisfy the final_sha contract without introducing a distinct publication_sha field or changing which report artifacts are committed.
